content:
JurisGrid National Debate Competition 2026

In a vibrant democracy, the right to protest and the responsibility to maintain public order often intersect, raising important constitutional questions.

JurisGrid invites students and legal enthusiasts to participate in a National Debate Competition on the theme:

⚖️ Freedom of Protest vs. Public Order: Where Should the State Draw the Line?

This debate is an opportunity to engage with one of the most significant constitutional issues of our time, sharpen your advocacy skills, and present well-reasoned legal arguments.

Date: 15 August 2026
Mode: Google Meet
Time: 4:00 pm
Exciting Prizes
E-Certificate for All Participants
High CV Value

If you're passionate about constitutional law, public policy, or debating, this is your chance to showcase your skills.

Interested in participating? Comment "Interested" below, and will share the registration details with you.
